evidence_quotes.quote: Video games are an important part of our cultural and creative history, and they deserve to be preserved rather than rendered inaccessible when online services are shut down.
Consumers should have the right to continue enjoying games they have purchased, even after official servers are retired.
purchased goods should remain with the person who bought them.

paragraphs.text: I strongly support the "Stop Destroying Video Games" initiative. Video games are an important part of our cultural and creative history, and they deserve to be preserved rather than rendered inaccessible when online services are shut down. Consumers should have the right to continue enjoying games they have purchased, even after official servers are retired. This initiative promotes digital preservation, respects consumer rights, and ensures that future generations can study, play, and appreciate these works. I urge lawmakers to include clear protections that prevent companies from deliberately disabling games or removing access without providing an alternative. Additionally, purchased goods should remain with the person who bought them. It is unfair that companies can revoke access to games or remove them entirely from digital libraries after purchase. When people pay for a product, they expect to keep it, just as with any physical good. Please help ensure that our digital entertainment heritage is not lost forever.
